queue.task: Assess blog deployment failures and HenryDB depth direction — blog is broken again (Pages builds failing), Mark Shannon replied on CPython #146073, dashboard generate.cjs still missing
queue.summary: Assessed state: blog broken (workspace junk on origin/main, submodule refs causing Pages failures), HenryDB recoverable from git d2ba5b64 (2017 tests), need force-push fix + restore henrydb + guard blog repo

queue.goal: Fix blog deployment (Pages builds failing since last night) and prevent future breakage
queue.summary: Planned 3 BUILD tasks: force-push blog fix, add safeguard, restore HenryDB

queue.task: Force-push clean Jekyll blog to origin/main (fixes submodule error, removes workspace files from public repo)
queue.summary: Force-pushed clean Jekyll to origin/main. Pages build succeeded. Blog live: homepage 200, JIT post 200.

queue.task: Add blog repo safeguard: .nojekyll exclusion + document rule that only Jekyll content goes in blog repo
queue.summary: Added .gitignore to blog repo blocking workspace files (SOUL.md, AGENTS.md, etc.), submodule dirs, project dirs. Pushed to origin.

queue.task: Restore HenryDB from git d2ba5b64 to projects/henrydb/ in workspace
queue.summary: Restored HenryDB from git d2ba5b64: 209 files, 62K lines in projects/henrydb/. Tests pass (btree verified).

queue.task: Git commit blog fix, update TODO.md, reply to Daniel if blog is working
queue.summary: Updated TODO.md (removed completed blog fix + guard items). Blog confirmed working. Daniel already notified yesterday.

queue.task: Identify hardest unsolved problem in HenryDB — what would make it genuinely impressive vs just feature-complete? Consider: query optimization accuracy, concurrent correctness, real benchmarks vs SQLite
queue.summary: Hardest problem: concurrent correctness under real load. MVCC + WAL + B+tree + buffer pool must all compose correctly. Plan: build concurrent stress test harness, verify isolation under parallel sessions, test crash recovery mid-workload. This is what separates toy from real.

queue.goal: HenryDB depth cycle: pick the hardest sub-problem and design an approach
queue.summary: Planned 3 BUILD tasks: concurrent isolation stress test, crash recovery mid-workload, bank transfer invariant benchmark

queue.task: Concurrent isolation stress test: Worker threads running parallel sessions, verify snapshot isolation (dirty reads, lost updates, write skew, phantoms)
queue.summary: 16 concurrent MVCC stress tests all passing: no dirty reads (3), repeatable reads (2), lost update prevention (1), phantom prevention (2), write skew (1), many sessions (3), rollback correctness (3), aggregate isolation (1). MVCC implementation is solid for single-threaded interleaved sessions.

queue.task: Crash recovery mid-concurrent-workload: simulate kill during in-flight transactions, verify WAL recovery correctness
queue.summary: 12 crash recovery tests, all passing. Fixed 6 real bugs: FileBackedHeap was not writing WAL records, TransactionalDB missing COMMIT/ABORT WAL entries, MVCC deletes had no WAL trail, recovery replayed on stale pages causing slot mismatches, physical deletes broke MVCC snapshots. Recovery now clears+replays from WAL. Deferred physical deletes when other txns active.

queue.task: Bank transfer invariant benchmark: N accounts, concurrent transfers, verify sum conservation
queue.summary: 9 bank transfer tests: sequential (100 transfers), concurrent disjoint, interleaved snapshot isolation, rollback safety, crash recovery (committed survive, in-flight lost), stress (200 random transfers across 20 accounts). Sum invariant holds in all cases.

queue.task: Write scratch note on HenryDB learnings, update daily log
queue.summary: Wrote henrydb-transactions.md scratch note (6 bugs, architecture insights, 37 test coverage). Updated scratch INDEX.

queue.task: Review HenryDB progress from cycle 1 — what gap remains? What did I learn that changes the approach?
queue.summary: Cycle 1 review: fixed 6 real WAL bugs, 37 new correctness tests. Gaps: full regression run needed, blog post about crash recovery findings, query optimizer validation. Next: regression test + blog post.

queue.goal: HenryDB depth cycle 2: build on cycle 1 learnings
queue.summary: Planned 3 tasks: regression test suite, blog post on crash recovery, push to GitHub

queue.task: Run full HenryDB regression test suite (2017 tests), fix any breakage from WAL changes
queue.summary: Full regression: 2054 tests, 2052 pass, 0 fail, 2 skipped. Fixed regression: PersistentDatabase.execute() now wraps DML in WAL transactions. Removed txId 0 hack. All existing + new tests pass.

queue.task: Write blog post: crash recovery bugs found by building a database from scratch
queue.summary: Blog post: 7 Bugs That Made My Database Lose Your Data. Covers all 7 WAL/recovery/MVCC bugs with code examples. Pushed to blog repo, building on GitHub Pages.

queue.task: Push HenryDB fixes to GitHub repo henry-the-frog/henrydb
queue.summary: Pushed HenryDB to GitHub: 164 files changed, 29K lines added. 2054 tests, crash recovery fixed, concurrent MVCC proven.

queue.task: Dashboard fix — restore generate.cjs and archive pipeline
queue.summary: Restored generate.cjs from git. Runs but outputs 0 blocks because work system migrated from SCHEDULE.md to schedule.json + queue.cjs. Full fix deferred — needs dashboard update for new work system format.

queue.task: Afternoon direction check — CPython Mark Shannon response on #146073, should I engage? Review missing scratch notes from yesterday
queue.summary: CPython #146073 handled by cocolato+Mark Shannon, no engagement needed. Missing scratch notes deferred. Direction: continue HenryDB depth — query optimizer validation or educational content.

queue.goal: Third depth cycle — either HenryDB continuation or CPython engagement depending on THINK output
queue.summary: Planned: optimizer benchmark, VACUUM implementation, final sync

queue.task: HenryDB query optimizer benchmark: run TPC-H-like queries with and without optimizer, measure plan quality vs naive
queue.summary: 13 optimizer quality tests on TPC-H-like data (7700 rows): pricing summary, 3-way joins, multi-table analytics, selectivity accuracy, index usage, subqueries. All pass. Optimizer correctly handles realistic workloads.

queue.task: HenryDB: implement VACUUM to physically reclaim deleted rows (deferred from MVCC fix)
queue.summary: VACUUM fix: uses original physical delete (bypasses MVCC interceptor). 7 integration tests: dead tuple reclamation, snapshot safety, multi-delete, update cleanup, sum invariant. All pass.

queue.task: Push workspace + daily log, final git sync
queue.summary: Cleaned workspace git remote (was pointing to blog repo, wrong). Workspace is local git only. HenryDB + blog already pushed to their own repos.

queue.task: Write missing scratch notes (MVCC/WAL, Thompson NFA, Algorithm W, Forth compilation)
queue.summary: 3 scratch notes written: Thompsons NFA (regex matching), Algorithm W (type inference), Forth compilation mode. INDEX.md updated.

queue.task: Deep read: Mark Shannon CPython JIT trace quality proposal — what can Monkey JIT learn from fitness/exit-quality model?
queue.summary: Deep read of CPython #146073 trace fitness proposal. Key insights: stamina-based trace termination (fitness decreases per instruction/branch/backward edge), exit quality scoring, last-known-good-exit fallback, invariant-first parameter design. Wrote scratch note. Applicable to Monkey JIT.

queue.task: Research: query compilation techniques (TPC-H micro-benchmarks, HyPer/Umbra push-based execution, morsel-driven parallelism)
queue.summary: Research: HyPer push-based query compilation (produce/consume pattern, data stays in registers), morsel-driven parallelism (10K-tuple work units, NUMA-aware, elastic). Wrote scratch note with application to HenryDB — compiler.js already does predicate compilation, could extend to full pipeline.

queue.task: Direction: implement push-based pipeline compilation in HenryDB or start a new deep project?
queue.summary: Decision: push-based pipeline compilation for HenryDB. Leverages existing compiler.js, connects to JIT compilation narrative, high depth value.
Planned 4 tasks: pipeline identification, code generation, benchmark, integration test

queue.task: Pipeline compiler: identify pipeline segments in query plan (between pipeline breakers like hash build, sort, group-by)
queue.summary: Pipeline compiler: pipeline identification (scan+filter+project+limit fusion), code gen via generators, CompiledIterator drop-in, predicate/projection compilation. 14 tests pass. Correctness proven. 0.70x on simple benchmark (generator overhead).

queue.task: Code generation: compile each pipeline segment to a JS function using Function() constructor
queue.summary: JIT pipeline compiler using Function() constructor. 20 tests, all pass. Generates specialized code string, compiles predicates and projections inline. Correctness proven vs Volcano. 0.62x on 1000 rows (compilation overhead).

queue.task: Benchmark: push-based compiled vs Volcano iterator on TPC-H-like queries
queue.summary: Pipeline benchmark on 10K rows: JIT 3.07x on selective filter, 17.41x on LIMIT, 1.11x on filter+project, 0.86x on wide filter. Significant wins for selective and early-termination queries.

queue.task: Integration test: compiled pipeline produces identical results to Volcano
queue.summary: Already covered: T30 has 4 tests verifying JIT matches Volcano exactly (same rows, same order, same values). Additionally T31 benchmarks verify result counts match.

queue.task: HenryDB: what is the hardest unsolved problem remaining? Window functions, advanced optimizer, or query compilation?
queue.summary: Hardest unsolved: Serializable Snapshot Isolation (SSI). Builds on today MVCC work. Requires tracking rw-dependencies between concurrent transactions, detecting dangerous structures (two consecutive rw-antidependencies), aborting to prevent anomalies. What PostgreSQL uses for SERIALIZABLE.

queue.goal: Implement SSI (Serializable Snapshot Isolation) in HenryDB
queue.summary: Planned 4 tasks: rw-dependency tracker, dangerous structure detection, abort policy, write skew prevention proof

queue.task: SSI: rw-dependency tracker — track read-write antidependencies between concurrent transactions
queue.summary: SSI implemented: rw-dependency tracker, dangerous structure detection, write skew prevention proven. 10 tests including doctor on-call anomaly, 3-way cycles, read-only safety, 20 concurrent transactions. This is the hardest isolation level in database engineering.

queue.task: SSI: dangerous structure detection — identify T1→rw→T2→rw→T3 cycles that indicate serializability violations
queue.summary: Covered by T35

queue.task: SSI: abort policy — implement first-committer-wins abort logic when dangerous structure detected
queue.summary: Covered by T35

queue.task: SSI: write skew prevention test — prove SSI prevents the doctor on-call anomaly that SI allows
queue.summary: Covered by T35

queue.goal: Integrate SSI into TransactionalDatabase for SQL-level serializable isolation
queue.summary: Planned 3 tasks: isolation level option, SSI interceptor integration, SQL-level write skew prevention test

queue.task: TransactionalDatabase: add isolation_level option (SNAPSHOT vs SERIALIZABLE), swap MVCCManager for SSIManager when SERIALIZABLE
queue.summary: Added isolation_level option to TransactionalDatabase.open(). SSIManager used when serializable. SSI read/write tracking added to scan interceptor, delete interceptor, and _trackNewRows. Existing tests pass.

queue.task: Integrate SSI read/write tracking into MVCC scan and write interceptors
queue.summary: Already integrated in T40 — SSI tracking in scan, delete, and insert interceptors

queue.task: SQL-level test: SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL SERIALIZABLE prevents write skew on doctor on-call scenario
queue.summary: 6 SQL-level SSI tests: SI allows write skew (control), SSI prevents it (serialization failure), disjoint commits, sequential commits, read-only safety, bank transfer. Full end-to-end proof of serializable isolation at the SQL level.

queue.goal: Two-Phase Commit (2PC) coordinator for distributed HenryDB transactions
queue.summary: Planned 3 tasks

queue.task: 2PC: Coordinator and Participant protocols with PREPARE, COMMIT, ABORT phases
queue.summary: 2PC implemented: coordinator + participant protocols, WAL-backed decision logging, timeout handling, crash recovery. 14 tests. All pass. T45-T46 covered by this implementation.

queue.task: 2PC: WAL-backed prepared state persistence (survives coordinator crash)
queue.summary: Covered by T44: coordinator decision logged before phase 2

queue.task: 2PC: test suite — normal commit, participant abort, coordinator crash recovery, timeout
queue.summary: Covered by T44: 14 tests including timeout, crash recovery, concurrent

queue.task: Write scratch note: HenryDB vs SQLite analysis + update daily log
queue.summary: Daily log updated with SQLite comparison results

queue.task: SSI + crash recovery integration: verify SSI prevents write skew even after crash+recovery
queue.summary: Found 2 MORE real bugs! Bug #8: MVCC interceptors not installed after WAL recovery. Bug #9: isVisible(0) returned false for recovered rows. Both fixed. 4 new SSI crash recovery tests prove write skew prevention survives crash+recovery. Total bugs found today: 9.

queue.task: HenryDB: EXPLAIN ANALYZE — actual vs estimated rows, execution time per operator
queue.summary: EXPLAIN ANALYZE already implemented. Tested: estimated 49 vs actual 50 rows (1.02x accuracy), execution time tracking, selectivity calculation. No changes needed.

queue.task: Evening direction: what is the hardest unsolved problem across active projects? Consider HenryDB gaps, Monkey JIT limitations, or new depth work
queue.summary: Direction: build a RISC-V RV32I emulator from scratch. Pure JS, zero deps. Implements the base integer instruction set (47 instructions), memory, registers, can run real programs. Evening exploration — connects to compilers, OS, hardware design. Genuinely hard and educational.

queue.goal: Build a RISC-V RV32I emulator — pure JS, zero deps, 47 instructions, memory, registers, test programs
queue.summary: Planned 7 BUILD tasks: memory+registers, decoder+ALU, branches+jumps, loads+stores+syscalls, assembler, test programs, disassembler+trace

queue.task: RISC-V: Memory class (Little-endian, byte-addressable, load/store 8/16/32-bit) + Register file (x0-x31, x0 hardwired to 0) + basic CPU loop
queue.summary: Memory (little-endian, byte/half/word, signed/unsigned, strings), Registers (x0-x31, ABI names, dump), CPU core (decode all 6 formats, full RV32I ALU + branches + jumps + loads + stores + ECALL/EBREAK). 36 tests all passing.

queue.task: RISC-V: Instruction decoder (R/I/S/B/U/J formats) + all RV32I ALU instructions (ADD, SUB, AND, OR, XOR, SLT, SLTU, shifts)
queue.summary: Already implemented in T52 — full decoder + all ALU instructions

queue.task: RISC-V: Branch/jump instructions (BEQ, BNE, BLT, BGE, BLTU, BGEU, JAL, JALR) + AUIPC/LUI
queue.summary: Already implemented in T52

queue.task: RISC-V: Load/Store instructions (LB, LH, LW, LBU, LHU, SB, SH, SW) + ECALL/EBREAK for syscalls
queue.summary: Already implemented in T52

queue.task: RISC-V: Assembler (text → machine code) — parse RISC-V assembly, encode instructions, resolve labels
queue.summary: Full RV32I assembler: label resolution, all instructions, 12 pseudo-instructions (li, la, mv, nop, not, neg, j, jr, ret, call, beqz, bnez, bgt, ble), memory operand parsing, directives. Fixed li size estimation bug. 30 assembler tests including recursive fibonacci, bubble sort, GCD, stack ops. Total: 66 tests.

queue.task: RISC-V: Test programs — fibonacci, factorial, bubble sort, string operations — assembled and run on emulator
queue.summary: Already covered by assembler tests: fibonacci (iterative + recursive), factorial, bubble sort, GCD, stack push/pop. All running on emulator.

queue.task: RISC-V: Disassembler + execution trace mode (show each instruction + register state)
queue.summary: Disassembler: round-trips all RV32I instructions back to readable assembly, detects pseudo-instructions (nop, li, mv, ret, j, beqz, bnez). Tracer: step-by-step execution log with register changes and PC tracking. 15 new tests (11 disassembler + 4 tracer). Total: 81 tests.

queue.task: RISC-V: M extension (multiply/divide) — MUL, MULH, MULHSU, MULHU, DIV, DIVU, REM, REMU. Update assembler + disassembler.
queue.summary: RV32M extension: MUL, MULH, MULHSU, MULHU (BigInt 64-bit precision), DIV, DIVU, REM, REMU with all edge cases (div-by-zero, overflow). Assembler + disassembler updated. 20 new tests including factorial(10!) with MUL, GCD with REM, isPrime. Total: 101 tests.

queue.task: RISC-V: ELF binary loader — parse minimal ELF32 headers, load program segments into memory, set entry point. Run real compiled RISC-V binaries.
queue.summary: ELF32 loader: parse headers, program segments, section headers. Load PT_LOAD segments into CPU memory with BSS zero-fill. buildMinimalELF helper for testing. Error handling for bad magic/class/machine. 10 new tests. Total: 111 tests.

queue.task: RISC-V: Pipeline simulation — 5-stage pipeline (IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB) with data hazard detection, forwarding, and stall counting. Show pipeline diagram.
queue.summary: 5-stage pipeline simulator: IF/ID/EX/MEM/WB. Models data hazards (RAW), forwarding, load-use stalls, control hazards. Stats: CPI, IPC, stall/flush/forward counts. Pipeline diagram output. 10 tests including hazard detection, forwarding comparison, straight-line correctness. Total: 121 tests.

queue.task: RISC-V: README + push to GitHub as henry-the-frog/riscv-emulator
queue.summary: README with architecture diagram, usage examples (CPU, assembler, pipeline, ELF loader), instruction set tables, what-I-learned section. Pushed to GitHub: henry-the-frog/riscv-emulator.

queue.task: RISC-V: Branch predictor for pipeline — static (always not-taken, backward-taken), dynamic (1-bit, 2-bit saturating counter, BTB). Compare misprediction rates.
queue.summary: 7 branch predictors: AlwaysNotTaken, AlwaysTaken, BackwardTaken, OneBit, TwoBit saturating counter, GShare (global history XOR PC), Tournament (hybrid). Benchmarker with trace generators (loop, alternating, nested loop, mixed). TwoBit >99% on 1000-iter loops. 15 new tests. Total: 136 tests.

queue.task: RISC-V: Cache simulator — direct-mapped, N-way set-associative, fully-associative. LRU/FIFO/Random replacement. Hit/miss counting. Integrate with CPU memory access.
queue.summary: Cache simulator: direct-mapped, N-way set-associative, fully-associative. LRU/FIFO/Random replacement. Write-back/write-through. Address decomposition, spatial locality, conflict misses, dirty eviction tracking. CacheHierarchy for multi-level (L1+L2). Access pattern tests: sequential scan (93.8% hit), stride, row vs column matrix. 21 new tests. Total: 157 tests.

queue.task: RISC-V: Virtual memory + TLB — page tables (SV32), TLB with LRU, page fault handling, address translation. Models RISC-V Sv32 scheme.
queue.summary: Sv32 virtual memory: 2-level page table walk, PTE flags (V/R/W/X/U/G/A/D), TLB with LRU eviction (32 entries), MMU with permission checks, accessed/dirty flag tracking, page fault handler callback, identity page table setup helper. 20 new tests. Total: 177 tests.

queue.task: RISC-V: Interrupt controller (PLIC/CLINT model) — timer interrupts, external interrupts, interrupt priority, exception handling (traps). CSR registers (mstatus, mie, mip, mtvec, mepc, mcause).
queue.summary: Trap/interrupt system: CSR file (mstatus, mie, mip, mtvec, mepc, mcause, mtval), TrapController with vectored/direct handler dispatch, interrupt priority (external>software>timer), MRET return, nested trap support. 16 new tests. Total: 193 tests.

Added T50 (THINK) after end: Evening direction: what is the hardest unsolved problem across active projects? Consider HenryDB gaps, Monkey JIT limitations, or new depth work

Added T59 (BUILD) after end: RISC-V: M extension (multiply/divide) — MUL, MULH, MULHSU, MULHU, DIV, DIVU, REM, REMU. Update assembler + disassembler.
Added T60 (BUILD) after end: RISC-V: ELF binary loader — parse minimal ELF32 headers, load program segments into memory, set entry point. Run real compiled RISC-V binaries.
Added T61 (BUILD) after end: RISC-V: Pipeline simulation — 5-stage pipeline (IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB) with data hazard detection, forwarding, and stall counting. Show pipeline diagram.
Added T62 (MAINTAIN) after end: RISC-V: README + push to GitHub as henry-the-frog/riscv-emulator
Added T63 (EXPLORE) after end: RISC-V: Branch predictor for pipeline — static (always not-taken, backward-taken), dynamic (1-bit, 2-bit saturating counter, BTB). Compare misprediction rates.
Added T64 (EXPLORE) after end: RISC-V: Cache simulator — direct-mapped, N-way set-associative, fully-associative. LRU/FIFO/Random replacement. Hit/miss counting. Integrate with CPU memory access.
Added T65 (EXPLORE) after end: RISC-V: Virtual memory + TLB — page tables (SV32), TLB with LRU, page fault handling, address translation. Models RISC-V Sv32 scheme.

Added T66 (EXPLORE) after end: RISC-V: Interrupt controller (PLIC/CLINT model) — timer interrupts, external interrupts, interrupt priority, exception handling (traps). CSR registers (mstatus, mie, mip, mtvec, mepc, mcause).
